title: SI v2.2 Risk Analysis -- canonical schema
description: Source-of-truth for the RA report shape. (1) `outputColumns` enumerates the canonical 21-col OutputPropertyOrder + 4 engine-meta columns. (2) `domains` carries per-(SecurityDomain) Impact lookup tables -- engine consults these when a row has no platform-provided Impact. Category + Subcategory are set by the per-report KQL via `extend Category = ...` (either a static literal or a tostring(<source-field>) when the platform exposes a meaningful classifier like EG `FindingLabel` or MDC `recommendationCategory`).

Identity.rationale: Identity findings are policy-driven (no MFA, stale account, unverified SPN, etc.) -- the platform has NO `Impact` column. Engine resolves Impact via the static byCategory map below; query author sets Category as a literal in `extend Category = "<lit>"`.

impact.comment: Initial scores -- tune per tenant policy. Range 0-10.

Endpoint.rationale: Endpoint findings come from MDE / MDVM / EG. Impact = CVSS 0-10 from `Properties.raw.cvssScore` when the EG finding carries it. When the platform doesn't surface Impact (posture / hygiene reports), engine falls back to the static byCategory map. Query author sets Category either as a literal OR `tostring(FindingLabel)` for EG-driven reports.

Azure.rationale: Azure findings come from Defender for Cloud + EG. Impact = `Properties.raw.severity` (MDC severity 0-10) when present; static byCategory fallback otherwise. Query author sets Category as a literal OR `tostring(FindingLabel)` / `tostring(Properties.raw.recommendationCategory)`.

Publicip.rationale: Public IP findings -- Shodan-derived port + service exposure. Single Category vocabulary today.

Hygiene.rationale: Hygiene reports check the engine's own data-quality (orphan rate, drift, coverage). Low Impact -- operator awareness, not security findings.

RiskAnalysis.rationale: RA-self meta reports (engine-internal). Diagnostic, not finding.

outputColumns.extrasPolicy: Reports MAY emit additional report-specific columns (EdgeLabel, AppHttpAllowed, cmdb fields, etc.) -- engine preserves them AFTER the canonical 21 in the projected row (Calculate-RiskScore @ Invoke-RiskAnalysis.ps1:1844).

engineNotes.categoryResolution: Per-report query OWNS Category and Subcategory via `extend Category = ...`. Two patterns: (a) STATIC literal: `extend Category = "<lit>"` (used by Identity / Hygiene / posture reports). (b) PLATFORM-DERIVED: `extend Category = tostring(FindingLabel)` for EG-finding reports / `extend Category = tostring(Properties.raw.recommendationCategory)` for MDC reports. Engine NEVER overrides Category from a hidden source-field map -- if you see something unexpected, it's the query speaking.
engineNotes.impactResolution: Engine resolves Impact in this priority order: (1) per-row `Impact` column from the query (CVSS / MDC severity numeric, 0-10), (2) static `domains.<SecurityDomain>.impact.byCategory[<row.Category>]`, (3) `domains.<SecurityDomain>.impact.default`. Result is normalized to a 0-10 real before SecuritySeverity mapping. The byCategory map is the SI authoring policy; tune scores per tenant by editing this JSON.
engineNotes.scoringOverride: RiskFactor_Consequence / RiskFactor_Probability / RiskFactor_Weight are still per-report (extends in the query). The static Impact map only provides the BASE value when the platform didn't surface one.
